department: David R. Cheriton School of Computer Science

courses.name: Introduction to Computing Through Applications
topics: Spreadsheets: cells, formulas, functions, relative/absolute references, charts, data sorting/filtering
Internet: history, infrastructure, protocols (HTTP, FTP, SMTP), IP addresses, DNS
WWW: web pages, hyperlinks, browsers, URLs, web servers
HTML: tags, elements, attributes, headings, paragraphs, links, images, lists, tables, forms
XML: syntax, elements, attributes, well-formed documents, namespaces, schemas, XSLT
Web Search Engines: crawling, indexing, ranking algorithms (PageRank), boolean queries, relevance feedback
Wikis: collaborative editing, version history, talk pages, wiki markup, MediaWiki
Data Collaboration: version control concepts, simultaneous editing, conflict resolution, cloud collaboration tools

courses.name: Introduction to Computer Programming 1
topics: Variables: naming conventions, data types (int, float, str, bool), assignment statements, type conversion
Conditionals: if, elif, else statements, comparison operators, logical operators (and, or, not), nested conditionals
Loops: while loops, for loops, range function, break, continue, nested loops, loop invariants
Functions: definition, parameters, return values, scope, local vs global variables, function composition, docstrings
Arrays: lists, indexing, slicing, appending, inserting, deleting, list comprehensions, mutability
Coding Style: PEP 8, naming conventions, indentation, comments, code organization, readability
Testing: unit tests, test cases, edge cases, assertions, debugging output, test-driven development basics
Debugging: print debugging, rubber duck debugging, systematic error isolation, common error types (syntax, runtime, logic)
2D Graphics: turtle graphics, coordinate systems, drawing shapes, colors, pen control, screen setup
Animation: frame-based animation, motion, timers, simple game loops, collision detection basics
Image Processing: pixel manipulation, RGB colors, brightness/contrast adjustment, simple filters (grayscale, invert, blur)

courses.name: Introduction to Computer Programming 2
topics: Text Processing: string methods (split, join, replace, find, strip), regular expressions (patterns, matching, substitution), parsing structured text (CSV, JSON)
Image Manipulation: pixel-level operations, color spaces (RGB, HSV), convolution filters (edge detection, sharpening, blur), image transformations (rotate, scale, crop)
Sound Processing: waveforms, sampling, amplitude, frequency, sound synthesis (sine, square, sawtooth), effects (echo, reverb, volume), spectrograms
Data Visualization: matplotlib, line plots, bar charts, scatter plots, histograms, customizing axes, labels, legends, colormaps, saving figures
User Interfaces: GUI frameworks, event-driven programming, buttons, text inputs, sliders, menus, layout managers, callbacks
Physical Simulation: Newtonian physics, gravity, friction, collision detection, particle systems, Euler integration, bouncing balls
Object-Oriented Programming: classes, objects, attributes, methods, constructors, encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, method overriding
Libraries: importing modules, package management (pip), documentation reading, API usage, third-party libraries (numpy, pillow, pygame)

courses.name: Principles of Computing for Science
topics: Functions: parameter passing, return values, scope, recursion, lambda functions, higher-order functions, function as first-class objects
Flow Control: conditionals, loops, exception handling (try/except), context managers, short-circuit evaluation
Lists: creation, indexing, slicing, list methods (append, extend, insert, remove, pop, sort), list comprehensions, nested lists
Arrays: numpy arrays, array operations (element-wise, broadcasting), reshaping, slicing, indexing, boolean masking
Numerical Accuracy: floating-point representation (IEEE 754), rounding errors, catastrophic cancellation, machine epsilon, comparing floats
Data Analysis: pandas DataFrames, Series, data cleaning, filtering, grouping, aggregation, merging, joining, pivot tables
Algorithms: searching (linear, binary), sorting (bubble, insertion, selection, merge, quick), algorithm complexity (Big-O, Big-Ω, Big-Θ)
Object-Oriented Programming Concepts: classes, objects, attributes, methods, inheritance, encapsulation, polymorphism, abstract classes

courses.name: Introduction to Computer Science 1
topics: Functional Programming: pure functions, no side effects, immutability, first-class functions, higher-order functions (map, filter, fold), function composition
Function Definition: lambda calculus influence, define syntax, parameters, function bodies, recursive definitions, conditional expressions (cond, if)
Recursion: base case, recursive case, structural recursion, generative recursion, tail recursion, recursion on numbers, lists, trees
Design & Testing: problem analysis, data definitions, function signatures, purpose statements, examples, test cases (check-expect, check-within)
Lists: empty list, cons (first/rest), list operations (length, append, reverse), list recursion, list abstraction
Trees: leaf nodes, internal nodes, tree recursion, tree traversal (pre-order, in-order, post-order), binary trees, n-ary trees
Data Abstraction: struct definitions, constructors, selectors, predicates, representation independence, opaque types, encapsulation

courses.name: Introduction to Computer Science 2
topics: Generative Recursion: divide and conquer, backtracking, recursion that doesn't follow structure, termination arguments, examples (quicksort, GCD, binary search)
Structural Recursion: recursion following data definitions, natural recursion pattern, accumulator-style recursion, examples (list processing, tree traversal)
Mutation: mutable data structures, assignment statements, set! operations, mutable lists, side effects, imperative updates
Imperative Language: Python syntax, statements vs expressions, sequential execution, state changes, assignment semantics, reference semantics
Primitive Types: integers, floats, strings, booleans, None, type conversion, type checking, type hints
I/O: print function, input function, string formatting (f-strings, format method), command-line arguments
Sequencing: statement ordering, compound statements, semicolons (vs newlines), block structure
Selection: if, elif, else, conditional expressions (ternary operator), nested conditionals, switch/match statements
Looping: while loops, for loops, range iteration, enumerate, zip, break, continue, else clause on loops, loop invariants
File I/O: open function, read modes (r, w, a, r+), read, readline, readlines, write, writelines, close, with context manager, file paths

courses.name: Designing Functional Programs
topics: Functional Programming: referential transparency, no side effects, expression evaluation, substitution model, pure functions
Syntax & Semantics: Racket syntax (S-expressions), parentheses, quote, quasiquote, unquote, evaluation rules, lexical scope
Substitution Model: beta-reduction, name binding, environment passing, substitution rules, lambda calculus basis
Design Recipes: data definitions, function signatures, purpose statements, examples, test cases, templates, coding from design
Testing: check-expect, check-within, test suites, test-driven development, property-based testing, random testing
Linear & Nonlinear Data Structures: lists (linear), trees (binary, general, n-ary), graphs, DAGs, hash tables, sets
Recursive Data Definitions: self-referential definitions, constructors, selectors, predicates, well-formed data, generative recursion
Abstraction: higher-order functions (map, filter, foldl, foldr), lambda abstraction, functional composition, parametric polymorphism
Encapsulation: modules, provide, require, information hiding, opaque types, interfaces, signatures
Generative Recursion: divide and conquer, backtracking, branch and bound, examples (quicksort, merge sort, Sudoku solver)
Historical Context: lambda calculus (Church), Lisp (McCarthy), Scheme, Racket, evolution of functional programming

courses.name: Elementary Algorithm Design and Data Abstraction
topics: Algorithm Design & Analysis: asymptotic analysis (Big-O, Ω, Θ), worst-case vs average-case, complexity classes (constant, logarithmic, linear, quadratic, exponential)
Imperative Programming: C programming, statements, expressions, control flow, mutable state, pointer semantics, memory model
Iterative & Recursive Sorting: insertion sort (iterative), selection sort, bubble sort, merge sort (recursive divide/conquer), quicksort (in-place partitioning)
Lists: singly linked lists, node structure (data, next), list traversal, insertion, deletion, reversal, searching
Stacks: LIFO operations (push, pop, top), array-based implementation, linked-list implementation, applications (balanced parentheses, expression evaluation)
Queues: FIFO operations (enqueue, dequeue, front), array-based (circular buffer), linked-list implementation (head/tail pointers)
Trees: binary trees, tree traversal (pre-order, in-order, post-order, level-order), binary search trees (insert, search, delete, min, max)
Abstract Data Types: interface specification, implementation hiding, invariants, representation invariants, abstraction functions
Memory Management: malloc, free, calloc, realloc, dynamic allocation, memory leaks, dangling pointers, garbage collection concepts, stack vs heap

courses.name: Tools and Techniques for Software Development
topics: Linux Command Line: navigation (cd, ls, pwd), file operations (cp, mv, rm, touch, mkdir, rmdir), file permissions (chmod, chown), processes (ps, kill, top), pipes (|), redirection (> , <, >>), grep, find, man pages
Shell Scripting: bash scripts, shebang (#!), variables, command substitution, conditionals (if, test), loops (for, while), functions, exit codes, debugging scripts
Version Control (Git): init, add, commit, status, log, diff, branch, checkout, merge, rebase, remote (clone, push, pull, fetch), resolving conflicts, .gitignore
Testing: unit testing frameworks (CUnit, Check), test fixtures, assertions, test coverage, mocking, integration tests, regression tests
Debugging: gdb (breakpoints, watchpoints, step, next, continue, backtrace, print), valgrind (memory leak detection, profiling), core dumps
IDEs: VS Code, CLion, Eclipse, features (syntax highlighting, auto-completion, refactoring, integrated debugging, version control integration)
Build Automation: make, Makefiles (targets, dependencies, rules, variables, automatic variables), cmake, autotools, incremental builds

courses.name: Programming Principles
topics: Programming Concepts: C programming, procedural abstraction, modular design, information hiding, separation of concerns
Procedures & Parameter Passing: stack frames, call by value, call by reference (using pointers), parameter evaluation order, function prototypes
Arrays & Structures: array indexing, pointer arithmetic, multidimensional arrays, struct definitions, struct fields, alignment, padding, nested structures
Recursion: recursive functions, base cases, recursive cases, recursion depth, stack overflow, converting recursion to iteration, tail recursion optimization
Sorting: quicksort (in-place, pivot selection, partitioning), merge sort (external memory), heap sort (binary heap), radix sort (non-comparison)
Pointers: pointer variables, address-of (&), dereference (*), null pointers, pointer arithmetic, function pointers, void pointers, double pointers
Dynamic Structures: linked lists (singly, doubly, circular), trees (BST, AVL), hash tables (chaining, open addressing), graphs (adjacency list/matrix)
Space/Time Analysis: algorithm complexity, space complexity (stack, heap), time complexity, big-O notation, recurrence relations, master theorem
Design Methodologies: top-down design, bottom-up design, stepwise refinement, modular decomposition, interface design, invariants

courses.name: Introduction to Data Abstraction and Implementation
topics: Software Abstractions: abstraction layers, abstraction boundaries, opaque types, interface vs implementation, separation of concerns
Encapsulation & Modularity: C++ classes, access specifiers (public, private, protected), friend functions, modules (C++20), namespace
Classes & Interfaces: class definition, member functions, data members, constructors, destructors, copy/move semantics, interface classes (pure virtual)
Object Instantiation: stack vs heap allocation (new/delete), constructors (default, parameterized, copy, move), RAII, initialization lists
Recursion: recursive functions on ADTs, recursion depth, tail recursion, iterative alternatives, recursive data structures
ADTs (Sequences, Stacks, Queues, Trees): sequence ADT (vector, list), stack (LIFO), queue (FIFO), deque, priority queue, binary tree, BST
Linked Structures: singly linked lists, doubly linked lists, circular lists, sentinel nodes, list operations (insert, delete, search, reverse)
Arrays: static arrays, dynamic arrays, std::vector, amortized analysis, resizing strategies (geometric growth), array vs linked list trade-offs
Memory Models: stack memory (automatic storage), heap memory (dynamic storage), static storage, memory lifetimes, allocation/deallocation
Dynamic Memory Management: new, delete, new[], delete[], placement new, smart pointers (unique_ptr, shared_ptr, weak_ptr), custom allocators

courses.name: Designing Functional Programs (Advanced Level)
topics: Functional Programming: referential transparency, no side effects, expression evaluation, substitution model, pure functions
Syntax & Semantics: Racket syntax (S-expressions), parentheses, quote, quasiquote, unquote, evaluation rules, lexical scope
Substitution Model: beta-reduction, name binding, environment passing, substitution rules, lambda calculus basis
Design Recipes: data definitions, function signatures, purpose statements, examples, test cases, templates, coding from design
Testing: check-expect, check-within, test suites, test-driven development, property-based testing, random testing
Linear & Nonlinear Data Structures: lists (linear), trees (binary, general, n-ary), graphs, DAGs, hash tables, sets
Recursive Data Definitions: self-referential definitions, constructors, selectors, predicates, well-formed data, generative recursion
Abstraction: higher-order functions (map, filter, foldl, foldr), lambda abstraction, functional composition, parametric polymorphism
Encapsulation: modules, provide, require, information hiding, opaque types, interfaces, signatures
Generative Recursion: divide and conquer, backtracking, branch and bound, examples (quicksort, merge sort, Sudoku solver)
Historical Context: lambda calculus (Church), Lisp (McCarthy), Scheme, Racket, evolution of functional programming
Advanced Functional Programming: pure functional paradigm, lazy evaluation, infinite data structures, monads, functors, applicative functors
Advanced Recursion: mutual recursion, nested recursion, higher-order recursion, primitive recursion, primitive recursive functions, Ackermann function
Continuations: continuation-passing style (CPS), call/cc, first-class continuations, coroutines, control flow reification, backtracking
Macros: define-syntax, syntax-rules, syntax-case, hygienic macros, macro expansion, compile-time metaprogramming, pattern matching
Type Systems: static typing, type inference, parametric polymorphism, algebraic data types, sum types, product types, type classes, dependent types
Program Verification: formal specification, preconditions, postconditions, loop invariants, termination proofs, Hoare logic, model checking

courses.name: Elementary Algorithm Design and Data Abstraction (Advanced Level)
topics: Algorithm Design & Analysis: asymptotic analysis (Big-O, Ω, Θ), worst-case vs average-case, complexity classes (constant, logarithmic, linear, quadratic, exponential)
Imperative Programming: C programming, statements, expressions, control flow, mutable state, pointer semantics, memory model
Iterative & Recursive Sorting: insertion sort (iterative), selection sort, bubble sort, merge sort (recursive divide/conquer), quicksort (in-place partitioning)
Lists: singly linked lists, node structure (data, next), list traversal, insertion, deletion, reversal, searching
Stacks: LIFO operations (push, pop, top), array-based implementation, linked-list implementation, applications (balanced parentheses, expression evaluation)
Queues: FIFO operations (enqueue, dequeue, front), array-based (circular buffer), linked-list implementation (head/tail pointers)
Trees: binary trees, tree traversal (pre-order, in-order, post-order, level-order), binary search trees (insert, search, delete, min, max)
Abstract Data Types: interface specification, implementation hiding, invariants, representation invariants, abstraction functions
Memory Management: malloc, free, calloc, realloc, dynamic allocation, memory leaks, dangling pointers, garbage collection concepts, stack vs heap
Advanced Algorithm Design: randomized algorithms, approximation algorithms, parallel algorithms, online algorithms, competitive analysis
Advanced Data Structures: red-black trees, B-trees, skip lists, Fibonacci heaps, disjoint-set union (union-find), segment trees, Fenwick trees, suffix trees
Algorithm Analysis: amortized analysis (aggregate, accounting, potential methods), recurrence solving (substitution, recursion tree, master theorem, Akra-Bazzi)
Memory Optimization: cache-oblivious algorithms, spatial/temporal locality, cache efficiency, false sharing, memory alignment, padding optimization
Advanced Recursion: divide and conquer optimization, recursion with memoization (dynamic programming), recursive backtracking with pruning, tail call optimization

courses.name: Concepts for Advanced Computer Usage
topics: Operating Systems (User View): OS functions (process management, memory management, file systems, I/O), user interfaces (CLI, GUI), system calls, file organization, directories, permissions
Hardware Design: CPU components (ALU, CU, registers), memory hierarchy (cache, RAM, disk), I/O buses, interrupts, DMA, clock speed, Moore's Law
Social Media: platforms (Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, TikTok), algorithms (feed ranking, recommendation, trending), echo chambers, filter bubbles, misinformation
Technology & Society: digital divide, privacy concerns, surveillance capitalism, intellectual property (copyright, patents, open source), ethical computing, professional responsibility

courses.name: Introduction to Computers and Computer Systems
topics: Computer Architecture: von Neumann architecture, Harvard architecture, instruction set architecture (ISA), RISC vs CISC, addressing modes, instruction formats
Organization: datapath, control unit, ALU, registers (general purpose, special purpose, status), buses (data, address, control), clock, cycles
System Services: interrupts (hardware, software), exceptions, trap handlers, system calls, boot process, BIOS/UEFI, device drivers, firmware
Software: system software (OS, compilers, assemblers, linkers, loaders), application software, middleware, firmware, virtualization
Processors: pipelining (5-stage: IF, ID, EX, MEM, WB), hazards (structural, data, control), forwarding, stalling, branch prediction, superscalar, out-of-order execution

Memory: memory hierarchy (register, cache L1/L2/L3, RAM, disk), SRAM vs DRAM, cache mapping (direct, associative, set-associative), cache policies (write-through, write-back), virtual memory (paging, segmentation, TLB)
I/O Devices: polling, interrupt-driven I/O, DMA, memory-mapped I/O, port-mapped I/O, device controllers, storage devices (HDD, SSD), input devices (keyboard, mouse), output devices (display, printer)
Performance: execution time, throughput, CPI (cycles per instruction), IPC, MIPS, MFLOPS, Amdahl's Law, speedup, benchmarks (SPEC, Dhrystone)

courses.name: Algorithmic Problem Solving
topics: Problem Specification: input/output specification, preconditions, postconditions, edge cases, invariants, problem constraints, boundary conditions
Algorithmic Paradigms: brute force, divide and conquer, decrease and conquer, transform and conquer, greedy, dynamic programming, backtracking, branch and bound
Analysis: time complexity (best, worst, average), space complexity, asymptotic analysis (Big-O, Ω, Θ), recurrence relations, complexity classes (P, NP)
Implementation: algorithm to code translation, data structure selection, optimization techniques, trade-offs (time vs space, simplicity vs efficiency)
Exhaustive Search: brute force, generate and test, combinatorial generation (permutations, combinations, subsets), state space search
Divide & Conquer: splitting, recursive solving, combining, master theorem, examples (merge sort, quicksort, binary search, Karatsuba multiplication, Strassen's algorithm)
Greedy Algorithms: locally optimal choices, exchange argument, optimal substructure, matroids, examples (activity selection, fractional knapsack, Huffman coding, Dijkstra, Prim, Kruskal)

Dynamic Programming: overlapping subproblems, optimal substructure, memoization vs tabulation, state definition, transition function, examples (fibonacci, knapsack, LCS, matrix chain multiplication, edit distance, rod cutting)

courses.name: Data Types and Structures
topics: Top-Down Design: problem decomposition, functional decomposition, stepwise refinement, modular design, interface specification, abstraction barriers
Representation-Independent Data Types: abstract data types, representation invariants, abstraction functions, implementation hiding, information encapsulation
Lists: sequence ADT, array-based lists (Python list), linked lists (singly, doubly), list operations (access, insert, delete, search, sort), iterator pattern
Sets: mathematical set ADT, operations (union, intersection, difference, subset), implementations (bit vectors, hash sets, tree sets), membership test
Mappings: dictionary ADT, key-value pairs, operations (lookup, insert, delete), implementations (hash tables, binary search trees, association lists)
Trees: tree ADT, terminology (root, leaf, parent, child, depth, height), binary trees, n-ary trees, tree traversal (DFS: pre/in/post-order, BFS: level-order), expression trees, parse trees
Data Representation Selection: trade-offs (time vs space, memory vs disk, sequential vs random access), representation choices based on operations, empirical evaluation

courses.name: Data Structures and Data Management
topics: Priority Queues: heap data structure (binary heap, binomial heap, Fibonacci heap), operations (insert, extract-min, decrease-key, merge), heapify, heapsort
Sorting: comparison-based sorts (insertion, selection, bubble, merge, quick, heap), non-comparison sorts (counting sort, radix sort, bucket sort), stability, in-place, adaptive
Dictionaries: hash tables (hash functions, collision resolution: chaining, open addressing, double hashing), binary search trees (unbalanced), self-balancing BSTs (AVL, red-black, treap, splay)
Data Structures for Text Processing: tries (prefix trees), suffix trees, suffix arrays, palindrome trees, Aho-Corasick automaton, Burrows-Wheeler transform
Algorithm Performance Analysis: worst-case, average-case, amortized analysis, recurrence relations, asymptotic notation, master theorem, lower bounds

courses.name: Data Structures and Data Management (Enriched)
topics: Priority Queues: heap data structure (binary heap, binomial heap, Fibonacci heap), operations (insert, extract-min, decrease-key, merge), heapify, heapsort
Sorting: comparison-based sorts (insertion, selection, bubble, merge, quick, heap), non-comparison sorts (counting sort, radix sort, bucket sort), stability, in-place, adaptive
Dictionaries: hash tables (hash functions, collision resolution: chaining, open addressing, double hashing), binary search trees (unbalanced), self-balancing BSTs (AVL, red-black, treap, splay)
Data Structures for Text Processing: tries (prefix trees), suffix trees, suffix arrays, palindrome trees, Aho-Corasick automaton, Burrows-Wheeler transform
Algorithm Performance Analysis: worst-case, average-case, amortized analysis, recurrence relations, asymptotic notation, master theorem, lower bounds
Enriched Data Structures: van Emde Boas trees (priority queue for integer keys), fusion trees (integer search), y-fast tries, x-fast tries, persistent data structures (partial, full persistence)
Advanced Algorithm Analysis: lower bounds (decision trees, adversary arguments), information-theoretic bounds, average-case analysis with distributional assumptions, smoothed analysis

Data Management: external memory algorithms (B-trees, B+ trees, external sorting, merge sort with k-way merging), cache-oblivious algorithms (optimal cache complexity, matrix multiplication, scanning)
Amortized Analysis: aggregate method, accounting method (potential function), banker's view, physicist's view, examples (dynamic array resizing, splay trees, Fibonacci heaps, union-find)

courses.name: Foundations of Sequential Programs
topics: Machine Architecture: CPU, registers (general purpose, special purpose: PC, SP, IR), memory (code, data, stack, heap), ALU, instruction cycle (fetch-decode-execute), data paths
Assemblers: assembly language syntax (labels, directives, opcodes, operands), one-pass assembler, two-pass assembler (symbol table, address resolution), macro assemblers, absolute vs relocatable code

Programming Language Specification & Translation: syntax (BNF, EBNF, parse trees), semantics (operational, denotational, axiomatic), translation phases (lexical analysis, parsing, semantic analysis, optimization, code generation)

Linkers & Loaders: object files (symbol tables, relocation entries), linking (static linking, dynamic linking), relocation (link-time, load-time), symbol resolution, address binding, library linking (static libraries (.a), shared libraries (.so, .dll)), position-independent code (PIC)

Block-Structured Languages: lexical scoping, nested scopes, visibility, name resolution, block syntax (begin/end, { }), declaration regions, scope rules (static vs dynamic scoping, lexical scoping, deep binding, shallow binding)

Parameter Passing: pass by value, pass by reference (C++ &), pass by value-result (copy-in/copy-out), pass by name (thunks, call-by-name, Algol), pass by need (lazy evaluation, thunks with memoization, Haskell), evaluation strategies (applicative vs normal order)

Language Comparison: C (imperative, manual memory management), C++ (multi-paradigm), Java (OOP, GC), Python (dynamic, interpreted), Scheme/OCaml/Haskell (functional), Ada (block-structured), Algol (nested functions)

courses.name: Foundations of Sequential Programs (Enriched)
topics: Machine Architecture: CPU, registers (general purpose, special purpose: PC, SP, IR), memory (code, data, stack, heap), ALU, instruction cycle (fetch-decode-execute), data paths
Assemblers: assembly language syntax (labels, directives, opcodes, operands), one-pass assembler, two-pass assembler (symbol table, address resolution), macro assemblers, absolute vs relocatable code

Programming Language Specification & Translation: syntax (BNF, EBNF, parse trees), semantics (operational, denotational, axiomatic), translation phases (lexical analysis, parsing, semantic analysis, optimization, code generation)

Linkers & Loaders: object files (symbol tables, relocation entries), linking (static linking, dynamic linking), relocation (link-time, load-time), symbol resolution, address binding, library linking (static libraries (.a), shared libraries (.so, .dll)), position-independent code (PIC)

Block-Structured Languages: lexical scoping, nested scopes, visibility, name resolution, block syntax (begin/end, { }), declaration regions, scope rules (static vs dynamic scoping, lexical scoping, deep binding, shallow binding)

Parameter Passing: pass by value, pass by reference (C++ &), pass by value-result (copy-in/copy-out), pass by name (thunks, call-by-name, Algol), pass by need (lazy evaluation, thunks with memoization, Haskell), evaluation strategies (applicative vs normal order)

Language Comparison: C (imperative, manual memory management), C++ (multi-paradigm), Java (OOP, GC), Python (dynamic, interpreted), Scheme/OCaml/Haskell (functional), Ada (block-structured), Algol (nested functions)

Enriched Machine Architecture: out-of-order execution, speculative execution, branch prediction, SIMD (vector instructions), VLIW, superscalar architecture, instruction-level parallelism, Tomasulo's algorithm
Advanced Assemblers: macro processors (macro expansion, macro parameters, nested macros), conditional assembly, advanced directive handling, link-time optimization (LTO)

Compiler Fundamentals: intermediate representations (three-address code, SSA form, CFG, AST), basic blocks, control flow analysis, data flow analysis (reaching definitions, live variables, available expressions), register allocation (graph coloring, linear scan), instruction selection (tree rewriting, peephole optimization), optimization techniques (constant propagation, common subexpression elimination, loop invariant code motion, strength reduction, dead code elimination)

courses.name: Logic and Computation
topics: Propositional Logic: syntax (atomic propositions, connectives: ∧, ∨, ⊕, →, ↔, ¬), semantics (truth tables), satisfiability, validity, entailment, equivalence, normal forms (CNF, DNF), resolution, Horn clauses

Predicate Logic: syntax (terms, predicates, quantifiers: ∀, ∃, functions), semantics (domains, interpretations, variable assignments), well-formed formulas, bound/free variables, prenex normal form, Skolemization

Formal Reasoning: natural deduction (introduction/elimination rules for each connective), sequent calculus, Hilbert systems, resolution (unification, factoring, paramodulation), soundness, completeness

Computability: Turing machines (deterministic, nondeterministic, multi-tape), Church-Turing thesis, decidability, semi-decidability, reduction, undecidable problems (halting problem, Entscheidungsproblem, Post correspondence problem), Rice's theorem
Godel's Incompleteness Theorem: first incompleteness theorem (consistency, ω-consistency, encoding of syntax), second incompleteness theorem (consistency statement), incompleteness consequences

Logic Applications: SAT solvers (DPLL, CDCL, conflict-driven learning, backtracking), SMT solvers (satisfiability modulo theories), theorem provers (Coq, Isabelle, HOL), logic programming (Prolog, Horn clauses, SLD resolution)

courses.name: Logic and Computation (Enriched)
topics: Propositional Logic: syntax (atomic propositions, connectives: ∧, ∨, ⊕, →, ↔, ¬), semantics (truth tables), satisfiability, validity, entailment, equivalence, normal forms (CNF, DNF), resolution, Horn clauses

Predicate Logic: syntax (terms, predicates, quantifiers: ∀, ∃, functions), semantics (domains, interpretations, variable assignments), well-formed formulas, bound/free variables, prenex normal form, Skolemization

Formal Reasoning: natural deduction (introduction/elimination rules for each connective), sequent calculus, Hilbert systems, resolution (unification, factoring, paramodulation), soundness, completeness

Computability: Turing machines (deterministic, nondeterministic, multi-tape), Church-Turing thesis, decidability, semi-decidability, reduction, undecidable problems (halting problem, Entscheidungsproblem, Post correspondence problem), Rice's theorem
Godel's Incompleteness Theorem: first incompleteness theorem (consistency, ω-consistency, encoding of syntax), second incompleteness theorem (consistency statement), incompleteness consequences

Logic Applications: SAT solvers (DPLL, CDCL, conflict-driven learning, backtracking), SMT solvers (satisfiability modulo theories), theorem provers (Coq, Isabelle, HOL), logic programming (Prolog, Horn clauses, SLD resolution)

Enriched Logic: modal logic (Kripke semantics, necessity, possibility, modal axioms S4, S5, K), temporal logic (LTL, CTL, CTL*, always, eventually, next, until, release), intuitionistic logic (Brouwer-Heyting-Kolmogorov interpretation, no excluded middle, Heyting algebra), linear logic (resource-sensitive, exponential operators, linear implication)

Advanced Formal Reasoning: cut elimination, Craig interpolation theorem, Beth definability theorem, Herbrand's theorem, unification algorithms (Robinson's algorithm, Martelli-Montanari), resolution strategies (unit resolution, input resolution, linear resolution, SLD resolution), paramodulation, superposition

Computability Theory: recursion theory (primitive recursive functions, μ-recursive functions, partial recursive functions), arithmetical hierarchy (Σ_n, Π_n, Δ_n), oracle machines, Turing degrees, many-one reductions, truth-table reductions, Kleene's recursion theorem

courses.name: Object-Oriented Software Development
topics: Object-Oriented Programming: classes, objects, inheritance (single, multiple, virtual), polymorphism (compile-time: overloading, templates; runtime: virtual functions, overriding), encapsulation (access specifiers: public, protected, private), abstraction (abstract classes, pure virtual functions, interfaces)

Software Design: SOLID principles (Single responsibility, Open-closed, Liskov substitution, Interface segregation, Dependency inversion), design patterns (creational: Singleton, Factory, Builder; structural: Adapter, Composite, Decorator; behavioral: Observer, Strategy, Command, Iterator, State, Template Method), UML diagrams (class, sequence, activity, use case)

Coding: C++ features (templates, exceptions, STL containers: vector, list, map, set; STL algorithms: sort, find, transform; lambda expressions, auto keyword, range-based for loops, move semantics, perfect forwarding), RAII (Resource Acquisition Is Initialization), copy/move constructors/assignment operators, rule of three/five/zero

Debugging: GDB with C++ (displaying objects, stepping into virtual functions, watchpoints, breakpoints with conditions), valgrind (memcheck for memory leaks, cachegrind for profiling), address sanitizer, undefined behavior sanitizer, static analysis tools (clang-tidy, cppcheck)

Testing: Google Test, Catch2, unit testing (test fixtures, assertions: EXPECT_EQ, ASSERT_THROW), mock objects (gmock), test coverage (gcov, lcov), test-driven development (red-green-refactor), continuous integration (CI/CD)
Documentation: Doxygen (documentation comments, tags: @param, @return, @throws, @brief), inline comments, README files, API documentation, design documents

Reusable Code: generic programming (templates, template specialization, variadic templates), STL algorithms and containers, code reuse through inheritance (base classes) and composition (has-a relationship), libraries (static, dynamic, header-only)

courses.name: Computer Organization and Design

Multiprocessors: symmetric multiprocessing (SMP), multicore processors, cache coherence (snooping protocols: MSI, MESI, MOESI, directory-based protocols), memory consistency models (sequential consistency, total store order, relaxed consistency, release consistency), parallel programming models (shared memory, message passing), Flynn's taxonomy (SISD, SIMD, MISD, MIMD), GPU architecture (CUDA cores, warp, thread blocks, global/shared memory, memory coalescing), SIMD instructions (MMX, SSE, AVX, NEON)

courses.name: Computational Methods in Business and Finance
topics: Floating-Point Arithmetic: IEEE 754 standard, precision (single, double), rounding modes (round to nearest, ties to even; round toward zero, round toward +∞, round toward -∞), floating-point errors (rounding error, cancellation error, absorption), machine epsilon, ulp (units in the last place), numerical stability (well-conditioned vs ill-conditioned problems), catastrophic cancellation, Kahan summation algorithm, compensated summation

Interpolation: polynomial interpolation (Lagrange form, Newton form, divided differences), Runge's phenomenon, Chebyshev nodes (minimize Runge's phenomenon), spline interpolation (linear spline, quadratic spline, cubic spline: natural, clamped, not-a-knot), Hermite interpolation, rational interpolation, barycentric interpolation

Portfolio Optimization: Markowitz mean-variance optimization (efficient frontier, minimum variance portfolio, tangency portfolio), risk-return trade-off, covariance matrix, expected returns, volatility, Sharpe ratio, capital asset pricing model (CAPM: beta, alpha, security market line, capital market line), diversification, constraints (long-only, leverage, cardinality constraints, sector constraints), quadratic programming, Black-Litterman model, robust optimization, risk budgeting, equal risk contribution

Contingent-Claims Valuation: options (call, put), derivatives (forwards, futures, swaps), Black-Scholes-Merton model (partial differential equation, closed-form solution for European options), Greeks (delta, gamma, vega, theta, rho), implied volatility, volatility smile/skew, exotic options (Asian options: arithmetic vs geometric averaging, lookback options: fixed vs floating strike, barrier options: knock-in, knock-out, binary/digital options, basket options), binomial options pricing model (Cox-Ross-Rubinstein, risk-neutral valuation, backward induction, early exercise for American options), trinomial tree, finite difference methods (explicit, implicit, Crank-Nicolson), Monte Carlo methods (variance reduction: antithetic variates, control variates, importance sampling, stratified sampling; quasi-Monte Carlo: low-discrepancy sequences, Sobol sequence, Halton sequence, Faure sequence; nested simulation, least squares Monte Carlo for American options: Longstaff-Schwartz)

Nonlinear Equations: root-finding algorithms (bisection method, Newton-Raphson method, secant method, false position/regula falsi, Brent's method), convergence analysis (linear, quadratic, superlinear convergence), stopping criteria, multiple roots, systems of nonlinear equations (Newton's method for systems, Jacobian matrix, Broyden's method, fixed-point iteration, Picard iteration)

Monte Carlo Methods: random number generation (pseudo-random generators: linear congruential generator, Mersenne Twister; true random generators), probability distributions (uniform, normal: Box-Muller transform, Marsaglia polar method, inverse CDF; lognormal, exponential, Poisson, binomial), variance reduction techniques (antithetic variates, control variates, importance sampling, stratified sampling, Latin hypercube sampling, moment matching), path generation (geometric Brownian motion, Euler-Maruyama discretization, Milstein method), simulation for risk measurement (value at risk: historical simulation, parametric VaR, Monte Carlo VaR; conditional value at risk/CVaR, expected shortfall), Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC: Metropolis-Hastings, Gibbs sampling)

Lattice Methods: binomial trees (Cox-Ross-Rubinstein, Jarrow-Rudd, Leisen-Reimer), trinomial trees, implied trees, adaptive mesh models, convergence analysis, early exercise handling (American options, Bermudan options), dividends handling (continuous, discrete), multi-asset trees

courses.name: Computer Applications in Business: Databases
topics: Data Management: data modeling (conceptual: entity-relationship diagrams, entities, attributes, relationships: one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-many; logical: relational model, tables, rows, columns, keys: primary key, foreign key, candidate key, superkey; physical: storage, indexing, partitioning), data integrity (entity integrity: primary key not null, unique; referential integrity: foreign key constraints; domain integrity: data types, check constraints; user-defined integrity), normalization (1NF: atomic values, no repeating groups; 2NF: no partial dependencies; 3NF: no transitive dependencies; BCNF: Boyce-Codd normal form; 4NF: no multi-valued dependencies; 5NF: join dependencies, lossless decomposition), denormalization (performance optimization, redundancy management, materialized views)

Data Storage: database architecture (storage engine, query processor, transaction manager, lock manager), file organization (heap files, sequential files, hash files, clustered files), record storage (fixed-length records, variable-length records, record headers, slotted pages), data pages (page layout: free space map, page directory), buffer management (buffer pool, page replacement policies: LRU, MRU, clock, FIFO, LFU; dirty pages, forced flushing, checkpointing), storage media (primary: RAM, cache; secondary: HDD, SSD; tertiary: tape, optical), RAID (RAID 0: striping, RAID 1: mirroring, RAID 5: distributed parity, RAID 6: double parity, RAID 10: striped mirrors), storage hierarchy

Data Selection: relational algebra (selection σ, projection π, join ⨝: theta join, equijoin, natural join, outer join: left, right, full, cross join ×, set operations: union ∪, intersection ∩, difference −, division ÷, rename ρ), SQL SELECT statement (clauses: SELECT, FROM, WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING, ORDER BY, LIMIT/OFFSET), joins (INNER JOIN, LEFT/RIGHT/FULL OUTER JOIN, CROSS JOIN, SELF JOIN), subqueries (correlated, non-correlated, EXISTS, IN, ANY, ALL, scalar subqueries), aggregate functions (COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX, GROUP_CONCAT), window functions (ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, LEAD, LAG, FIRST_VALUE, LAST_VALUE, NTILE, moving averages, cumulative sums), set operations (UNION, UNION ALL, INTERSECT, EXCEPT), query optimization (predicate pushdown, join reordering, subquery flattening, materialized view matching, cost-based optimization, index selection, statistics collection, query plan caching)

Data Presentation: views (virtual tables, updatable views, materialized views, view security, view dependencies), report generation (SQL reporting, reporting tools: Crystal Reports, JasperReports, SSRS), data visualization (charts: bar, line, pie, scatter, heatmap, treemap; dashboards, drill-down reports, pivot tables, pivot charts), OLAP (online analytical processing: cube, roll-up, drill-down, slice, dice, pivot), business intelligence tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker), data storytelling

Database Management Systems: DBMS architecture (client-server: two-tier, three-tier; multi-tier), popular DBMS (relational: MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server, SQLite; NoSQL: MongoDB: document; Cassandra: wide-column; Redis: key-value; Neo4j: graph; Elasticsearch: search engine), DBMS components (query processor: parser, optimizer, executor; storage engine: buffer manager, transaction manager, lock manager; catalog manager: metadata, system tables), administration (user management: CREATE USER, GRANT, REVOKE, roles; backup and recovery: full backup, incremental backup, point-in-time recovery; performance tuning: indexing strategies, query rewriting, configuration tuning, monitoring, slow query log; security: authentication, authorization, encryption at rest and in transit, auditing), ACID properties (Atomicity: all or nothing, commit, rollback; Consistency: integrity constraints, invariants; Isolation: concurrency control, transaction isolation levels; Durability: persistence after commit), concurrency control (locking: shared locks S, exclusive locks X, lock escalation; two-phase locking: expanding phase, shrinking phase, strict 2PL, rigorous 2PL; deadlock detection: wait-for graph, timeout; deadlock prevention: wound-wait, wait-die, no-wait; timestamp ordering: basic T/O, multiversion concurrency control MVCC; optimistic concurrency control: validation, read/write sets), recovery (log-based: undo logging, redo logging, undo/redo logging, ARIES: analysis, redo, undo phases; checkpoints: fuzzy, sharp, consistent; shadow paging), isolation levels (READ UNCOMMITTED: dirty reads, READ COMMITTED: non-repeatable reads, REPEATABLE READ: phantom reads, SERIALIZABLE: full isolation, snapshot isolation)

courses.name: Algorithms
topics: Divide & Conquer: recursion tree method, master theorem (case 1: f(n) = O(n^(log_b a - ε)), case 2: f(n) = Θ(n^(log_b a) * log^k n), case 3: f(n) = Ω(n^(log_b a + ε)) with regularity condition), Akra-Bazzi method (for non-uniform recurrences), examples (merge sort, quicksort, binary search, Karatsuba multiplication: O(n^log2 3), Strassen's matrix multiplication: O(n^log2 7), closest pair of points: O(n log n), fast Fourier transform FFT: O(n log n), maximum subarray sum: O(n log n) divide-and-conquer, convex hull: O(n log n), integer multiplication: Toom-Cook 3-way, integer multiplication: Schönhage-Strassen)

Recurrences: solving recurrences (substitution method, iteration method, recursion tree method, master theorem, Akra-Bazzi method), recurrence types (linear homogeneous: constant coefficients, characteristic equation; linear non-homogeneous: particular solution + homogeneous solution; divide-and-conquer: T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n); subtract-and-conquer: T(n) = T(n-1) + f(n)), common recurrences (binary search: T(n) = T(n/2) + O(1) → O(log n), merge sort: T(n) = 2T(n/2) + O(n) → O(n log n), quicksort worst-case: T(n) = T(n-1) + O(n) → O(n^2), quicksort average: T(n) = T(n/2) + T(n/2) + O(n) → O(n log n), Fibonacci: T(n) = T(n-1) + T(n-2) + O(1) → O(φ^n), Karatsuba: T(n) = 3T(n/2) + O(n) → O(n^(log2 3)), Strassen: T(n) = 7T(n/2) + O(n^2) → O(n^(log2 7)))

Greedy Algorithms: exchange argument (swap adjacent, bubble sort proof), optimal substructure, matroid theory (hereditary property, exchange property, greedy choice property), examples (activity selection: sort by finish time, O(n log n), fractional knapsack: sort by value/weight ratio, O(n log n), Huffman coding: binary tree, prefix codes, optimal merge patterns, Dijkstra's algorithm: single-source shortest paths, non-negative weights, O((V+E) log V) with binary heap, O(V log V + E) with Fibonacci heap, Prim's algorithm: minimum spanning tree, O((V+E) log V) with binary heap, O(E + V log V) with Fibonacci heap, Kruskal's algorithm: minimum spanning tree, union-find, O(E log V), job sequencing with deadlines, Egyptian fraction representation, coin change with canonical coin systems, minimum number of coins: greedy works for standard denominations, minimum spanning tree: cut property, cycle property, Borůvka's algorithm, scheduling to minimize lateness: earliest due date first, scheduling to minimize completion time: shortest processing time first)

Dynamic Programming: optimal substructure (recursive optimal solution from subproblems), overlapping subproblems (memoization, tabulation), state definition (dimensions: 1D: fib, 2D: knapsack, LCS, edit distance, matrix chain; 3D: DP on intervals, DP on trees), transition function (recurrence relation, base cases, DP table filling order: bottom-up, top-down with memoization), examples (Fibonacci: O(n) time O(1) space, 0/1 knapsack: O(nW) time O(W) space, unbounded knapsack: O(nW) time, coin change (minimum coins): O(n * amount), coin change (number of ways): O(n * amount), longest common subsequence LCS: O(mn) time O(min(m,n)) space, edit distance Levenshtein: O(mn) time O(min(m,n)) space, matrix chain multiplication: O(n^3) time O(n^2) space, longest increasing subsequence LIS: O(n log n) with patience sorting, rod cutting: O(n^2) time, optimal BST: O(n^3) time O(n^2) space, palindrome partitioning: O(n^2) time, word break: O(n^2) time, maximum subarray Kadane: O(n) time 1D DP, traveling salesman problem TSP: O(n^2 * 2^n) time with bitmask DP, DP on trees: tree diameter, tree independent set, tree DP with rerooting, DP on intervals: matrix chain, stone game, burst balloons, DP with bitmask: TSP, set cover, DP on DAGs: longest path, shortest path, count paths, DP with state compression, DP with convex hull trick, DP with divide and conquer optimization, DP with Knuth optimization, DP with monotone queue optimization, DP with slope trick)

Graph Search: graph representations (adjacency matrix: O(V^2) space, adjacency list: O(V+E) space, edge list, incidence matrix), BFS (breadth-first search: queue, O(V+E) time, shortest paths in unweighted graphs, level order traversal, bipartite graph checking), DFS (depth-first search: stack/recursion, O(V+E) time, topological sort: DFS-based, Kahn's algorithm, cycle detection in directed/undirected graphs, connected components, strongly connected components SCC: Kosaraju's algorithm, Tarjan's algorithm, path finding, articulation points/cut vertices: DFS low-link values, bridges: DFS tree edges, biconnected components, 2-edge-connected components, Eulerian path/circuit: Hierholzer's algorithm, Fleury's algorithm, Hamiltonian path (NP-complete), graph coloring: greedy coloring, Welsh-Powell algorithm, graph isomorphism, A* search (heuristic search, admissible heuristic, consistent heuristic, open set, closed set, f = g + h, Dijkstra as A* with h=0, best-first search), bidirectional search, iterative deepening depth-first search (IDDFS), uniform cost search (UCS), branch and bound, backtracking (N-Queens, Sudoku solver, graph coloring, Hamiltonian cycle, subset sum, knight's tour, rat in maze, generating permutations/combinations, pruning techniques: forward checking, constraint propagation, AC-3 algorithm, constraint satisfaction problems CSP: backtracking search, heuristics: minimum remaining values MRV, degree heuristic, least constraining value, forward checking, constraint propagation, arc consistency, MAC, min-conflicts algorithm for N-Queens, local search for CSP)

Backtracking: state space tree, pruning (bounding functions, feasibility pruning, optimality pruning), branch and bound (bounding: upper bound, lower bound, best-first search, LC branch and bound, FIFO branch and bound, LIFO branch and bound), examples (N-Queens: O(N!) time, permutations generation, combinations generation, subsets generation, Sudoku solver: backtracking with forward checking, graph coloring, Hamiltonian cycle, subset sum: O(2^(n/2)) meet-in-the-middle, knapsack: branch and bound, traveling salesman problem TSP: branch and bound with reduced cost matrix, partition problem, crossword puzzle, word search, regex matching, maze solving)

NP-Completeness: decision problems vs optimization problems, P (polynomial time: problems solvable in O(n^k)), NP (nondeterministic polynomial time: problems verifiable in polynomial time), NP-complete (NP-hard and in NP: hardest problems in NP), NP-hard (as hard as any NP problem, may not be in NP), co-NP (complements of NP problems), polynomial-time reduction (many-one reduction/Karp reduction, Turing reduction/Cook reduction), NP-complete problems (SAT: boolean satisfiability, 3-SAT: 3-CNF satisfiability, vertex cover, clique, independent set, Hamiltonian cycle, traveling salesman problem TSP decision version, subset sum, partition, graph coloring, 3-coloring, exact cover, set cover, knapsack decision version, bin packing, integer linear programming, longest path, dominating set, feedback vertex set, steiner tree, graph isomorphism status unknown), NP-completeness proofs (reduction from known NP-complete problems: 3-SAT to vertex cover, vertex cover to clique, 3-SAT to Hamiltonian cycle, Hamiltonian cycle to TSP, 3-SAT to subset sum, subset sum to partition, vertex cover to dominating set, 3-coloring reduction from 3-SAT, independent set from vertex cover by complement graph), Cook-Levin theorem (SAT is NP-complete: SAT ∈ NP, SAT is NP-hard), approximation algorithms (vertex cover: 2-approximation via maximal matching, traveling salesman problem TSP metric: Christofides algorithm 1.5-approximation, set cover: ln n approximation via greedy, bin packing: first-fit decreasing 11/9 approximation, knapsack: FPTAS fully polynomial-time approximation scheme via dynamic programming, k-center: 2-approximation, MAX-SAT: 2-approximation, MAX-CUT: 0.878-approximation via Goemans-Williamson, submodular maximization: (1-1/e)-approximation via greedy), parameterized complexity (fixed-parameter tractable FPT: vertex cover O(2^k * n), kernelization, problem kernels, W hierarchy: W[1]-complete, W[2]-complete, FPT vs W-hardness), polynomial hierarchy (Σ_k^P, Π_k^P, PH), randomized complexity classes (RP, co-RP, ZPP, BPP, PP, #P), quantum complexity classes (BQP, QMA)

courses.name: Numeric Computation for Financial Modeling
topics: Financial models: Black-Scholes-Merton, stochastic differential equations (SDEs), geometric Brownian motion, jump diffusion
Option pricing: European options, American options (early exercise), exotic options (barrier, Asian, lookback)
Finite difference methods: explicit, implicit, Crank-Nicolson for Black-Scholes PDE, stability (CFL condition), boundary conditions
Monte Carlo methods: path generation (Euler-Maruyama, Milstein), variance reduction (antithetic variates, control variates, importance sampling)
Binomial and trinomial trees: Cox-Ross-Rubinstein, risk-neutral valuation, convergence, early exercise for American options
Portfolio optimization: mean-variance optimization, efficient frontier, Sharpe ratio, Black-Litterman model
Risk management: value at risk (VaR), conditional VaR (CVaR), historical simulation, parametric VaR
Interest rate models: Vasicek, Cox-Ingersoll-Ross (CIR), Hull-White, Heath-Jarrow-Morton (HJM)

courses.name: Neural Networks
topics: Neuron models: perceptron, sigmoid, tanh, ReLU, leaky ReLU, softmax, activation functions
Feedforward networks: architecture, universal approximation theorem, depth vs width, dropout, batch normalization
Backpropagation: chain rule, gradient computation, vanishing/exploding gradients, automatic differentiation
Training optimization: SGD, momentum, Adam, RMSprop, learning rate schedules, weight initialization
Convolutional neural networks (CNN): convolution layers, pooling (max, average), stride, padding, architectures (LeNet, AlexNet, ResNet, Inception)
Recurrent neural networks (RNN): LSTMs, GRUs, sequence modeling, vanishing gradients, teacher forcing
Unsupervised learning: autoencoders (vanilla, variational, denoising), self-organizing maps, contrastive learning
Adversarial learning: generative adversarial networks (GANs), training stability (Wasserstein GAN, spectral normalization)

courses.name: Introduction to Machine Learning
topics: Supervised learning: regression (linear, polynomial, ridge, lasso), classification (logistic regression, k-NN, decision trees, SVMs)
Model selection: training/validation/test split, cross-validation, bias-variance trade-off, overfitting and underfitting
Ensemble methods: bagging (random forests), boosting (AdaBoost, gradient boosting, XGBoost), stacking
Unsupervised learning: clustering (k-means, hierarchical, DBSCAN, Gaussian mixture models), dimensionality reduction (PCA, t-SNE, UMAP)
Probabilistic methods: maximum likelihood estimation (MLE), maximum a posteriori (MAP), Bayesian inference, Gaussian processes
Kernel methods: kernel trick, support vector machines (linear, polynomial, RBF), kernel ridge regression
Evaluation metrics: accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, ROC curves, AUC, confusion matrix
Deep learning foundations: neural networks, backpropagation, optimization, regularization (dropout, batch norm)

courses.name: Computational Techniques in Biological Sequence Analysis
topics: Sequence alignment: Needleman-Wunsch (global alignment), Smith-Waterman (local alignment), affine gap penalties
Multiple sequence alignment: progressive alignment (ClustalW, MUSCLE), iterative methods, hidden Markov models (HMMs)
Database search: BLAST (Basic Local Alignment Search Tool), FASTA, E-value, scoring matrices (PAM, BLOSUM)
Sequence assembly: overlap-layout-consensus (OLC), de Bruijn graphs (for short reads), Eulerian paths
Phylogenetic tree reconstruction: distance-based (UPGMA, neighbor-joining), character-based (maximum parsimony, maximum likelihood, Bayesian)
Motif finding: consensus, position weight matrices (PWM), Gibbs sampling, expectation-maximization (EM)
Genome annotation: gene finding (ORF, splice sites), hidden Markov models for gene prediction (GENSCAN, Glimmer)
RNA secondary structure prediction: Nussinov algorithm, dynamic programming, free energy minimization, comparative methods

courses.name: Computational Vision
topics: Image formation: pinhole camera model, perspective projection, lens distortion, radiometry, color spaces (RGB, HSV, LAB)
Image processing: filtering (Gaussian, median, bilateral), edge detection (Sobel, Canny), morphological operations (erosion, dilation)
Feature detection: corners (Harris, FAST), blobs (LoG, DoG), SIFT, SURF, ORB, feature descriptors
Stereo vision: epipolar geometry, fundamental matrix, essential matrix, disparity estimation, depth from stereo
Motion analysis: optical flow (Lucas-Kanade, Horn-Schunck), motion segmentation, structure from motion
Object recognition: sliding window, HOG + SVM, R-CNN, YOLO, SSD, detection metrics (IoU, mAP)
Segmentation: thresholding, region growing, graph cuts, semantic segmentation (FCN, U-Net, Mask R-CNN)
3D reconstruction: structure from motion (SfM), multi-view stereo, SLAM (simultaneous localization and mapping)

courses.name: Statistical and Computational Foundations of Machine Learning
topics: Statistical learning theory: PAC learning, VC-dimension, sample complexity, uniform convergence, Rademacher complexity
Generalization bounds: Hoeffding's inequality, McDiarmid's inequality, margin bounds, stability bounds
Regularization: L1 (lasso), L2 (ridge), elastic net, group lasso, sparsity, model selection
Kernel methods: reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces (RKHS), kernel trick, Mercer's theorem, kernel selection
Support vector machines: hard margin, soft margin, hinge loss, SMO algorithm, multi-class SVM
Boosting: AdaBoost (exponential loss, margin theory), gradient boosting, XGBoost, CatBoost
Neural network theory: universal approximation, depth efficiency, lottery ticket hypothesis, neural tangent kernel (NTK)
Data-dependent bounds: Rademacher complexity, local Rademacher complexity, PAC-Bayesian bounds

courses.name: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
topics: Problem solving: state-space search (BFS, DFS, A*, IDA*), heuristics (admissible, consistent), constraint satisfaction (CSP, backtracking, AC-3)
Knowledge representation: propositional logic, first-order logic, ontologies, semantic networks, frames
Reasoning: inference (resolution, forward chaining, backward chaining), deduction, abduction, non-monotonic reasoning
Planning: STRIPS, PDDL, forward/backward search, partial-order planning, graphplan, hierarchical planning
Reasoning under uncertainty: probability, Bayesian networks (inference, learning, structure learning), hidden Markov models (HMMs), Kalman filters
Multi-agent systems: game theory (Nash equilibrium, minimax), cooperative vs competitive agents, negotiation, auctions, mechanism design
Natural language processing: parsing (CFG, dependency), part-of-speech tagging, named entity recognition, sentiment analysis, machine translation
AI ethics: fairness, bias, transparency, accountability, explainable AI (XAI), AI safety

courses.name: Introduction to Symbolic Computation
topics: Symbolic representation: expression trees, canonical forms, normal forms, simplification rules
Algebraic manipulation: polynomial arithmetic (addition, multiplication, division), Gröbner bases, Buchberger's algorithm
Symbolic differentiation: automatic differentiation (forward mode, reverse mode), differentiation rules, expression simplification
Symbolic integration: Risch algorithm, integration of rational functions, special functions (error function, elliptic integrals)
Equation solving: symbolic solution of linear systems (Gaussian elimination), polynomial equations (resultants, Gröbner bases)
Mathematical simplification: trigonometric identities, logarithmic identities, exponential simplification, algebraic simplification
Computer algebra systems: architecture of CAS (Maple, Mathematica, SymPy), term rewriting, pattern matching
Applications: automatic theorem proving, formal verification, scientific computing, education

courses.name: Introduction to Computer Graphics
topics: 2D graphics: rasterization, line drawing (Bresenham), polygon filling (scanline, flood fill), anti-aliasing, clipping (Cohen-Sutherland, Liang-Barsky)
3D transformations: translation, rotation (Euler angles, quaternions), scaling, perspective and orthographic projection, homogeneous coordinates
Rendering pipeline: vertex processing, rasterization, fragment processing, framebuffer operations
Shading: illumination models (Lambertian, Phong, Blinn-Phong), materials, textures (mapping, filtering, mipmapping)
Hidden surface removal: painter's algorithm, Z-buffer (depth buffer), BSP trees, occlusion culling
Ray tracing: primary rays, intersection tests (sphere, triangle, plane), reflection, refraction, shadows, acceleration structures (BVH, kd-tree, grid)
Global illumination: path tracing, photon mapping, radiosity, Monte Carlo integration
Graphics APIs: OpenGL (shaders, buffers, textures), Vulkan, DirectX, WebGL

courses.name: Advanced Topics in Computer Science
topics: Varies by offering: specialized advanced topics in CS research areas

courses.name: Information Systems Management
topics: IS strategy: alignment with business strategy, strategic information systems, competitive advantage through IT
IS planning and development: strategic planning, enterprise architecture, portfolio management, IT governance (COBIT, ITIL)
Decision support systems: business intelligence, analytics, data warehousing, OLAP, dashboards, data visualization
IT operations: service management (ITSM), incident management, problem management, change management, release management
IT security and risk management: risk assessment, compliance (SOX, GDPR, HIPAA), disaster recovery, business continuity
Enterprise systems: ERP (SAP, Oracle), CRM (Salesforce), SCM, knowledge management systems
IT outsourcing and vendor management: make vs buy, vendor selection, contract management, SLAs
Emerging technologies: cloud computing, big data, AI/ML in business, digital transformation, blockchain

courses.name: The Social Implications of Computing
topics: Privacy and surveillance: data collection, tracking, encryption, anonymity, de-anonymization, mass surveillance, GDPR
Security and cybercrime: hacking, malware, phishing, cyberterrorism, cyberwarfare, defense strategies
Intellectual property: software copyright, patents, open source licensing (GPL, MIT, Apache), Creative Commons
Professional ethics: ACM/IEEE code of ethics, whistleblowing, professional responsibility, conflicts of interest
AI and automation: job displacement, algorithmic bias, fairness, accountability, transparency (XAI), AI safety
Social media and society: echo chambers, filter bubbles, misinformation, fake news, content moderation, platform responsibility
Digital divide: access inequality, digital literacy, universal access, net neutrality
Computing and sustainability: energy consumption, e-waste, green computing, carbon footprint of data centers

courses.name: Team Project 1
topics: Project management: team formation, roles (project manager, technical lead, QA, developer), communication, conflict resolution
Project definition: problem identification, requirements elicitation, feasibility study, project scope, success criteria
Goal setting: SMART goals, milestones, deliverables, success metrics, key performance indicators (KPIs)
Risk assessment: risk identification, risk analysis (probability × impact), mitigation strategies, contingency planning
Project planning: work breakdown structure (WBS), Gantt charts, task dependencies, critical path, resource allocation
Prototyping: low-fidelity prototypes, wireframes, mockups, proof-of-concept, iterative refinement
Ethical and legal issues: intellectual property, licensing, data privacy, ethical considerations, compliance requirements
Documentation: project charter, requirements document, design document, meeting minutes, progress reports

courses.name: Team Project 2
topics: Project development: agile methodology (Scrum: sprints, daily standups, sprint planning, sprint review, retrospective), version control (Git)
Design alternatives: architectural decisions, technology selection, trade-off analysis, design reviews
Implementation: coding standards, code reviews, pair programming, continuous integration, documentation
Testing: unit testing, integration testing, system testing, acceptance testing, test automation, bug tracking
Results analysis: performance testing, usability testing, data analysis, comparison against requirements
Technical presentations: demo preparation, slide design, technical communication, stakeholder presentations
Project deliverables: final report, user manual, technical documentation, source code, deployment guide
Ethical/legal analysis: privacy impact assessment, security audit, licensing compliance, ethical review

courses.name: Multidisciplinary Studies in Computer Science
topics: Varies by offering: cross-disciplinary topics combining CS with other fields (biology, physics, economics, psychology, etc.)

courses.name: Readings in Computer Science
topics: Independent reading and literature review under faculty supervision

courses.name: Honours Thesis
topics: Independent research: research question formulation, literature review, hypothesis development
Methodology: experimental design, data collection, analysis techniques, validation methods
Implementation: software/hardware development, experimentation, data collection, iteration
Analysis and synthesis: results interpretation, discussion, conclusions, future work
Thesis writing: introduction, related work, methodology, results, discussion, conclusion, references
Presentation: thesis defense, research presentation, poster session, peer feedback
